title: Objects
description: Understand instantiating objects on the Stack vs the Heap, lifetimes, and object layouts in memory.
learningObjectives: Instantiate objects on the stack and understand automatic lifetimes
Allocate objects dynamically on the heap and prevent memory leaks
Explain the memory offset representation of object properties

sections.title: Object Allocations: Stack vs Heap
sections.content:
An object is an instance of a class loaded into active memory. Where it lives is critical for game performance:

1. **Stack Allocation**: Fast, automatic lifetime (deallocated when scope ends), localized cache layout. Ideal for short-lived transforms, traces, and calculations.
2. **Heap Allocation**: Slower, manual lifetime (requires `new` / `delete`), dynamically sized. Required for massive persisting objects like characters and level nodes. Heap memory must be managed carefully to avoid leaks.

sections.title: Object Allocations in Action
